message:
fix: historical salvage + count_sessions drop data on NULL agent_id

Two more code paths that silently dropped rows via INNER JOIN on agents:

1. import_historical_sources conv_sql: this rusqlite-backed path reads
   conversations out of a legacy bundle during historical salvage and
   imports them into the current database.  The legacy V1 schema
   declared agent_id as nullable, so valid source conversations with
   NULL agent_id were silently excluded by the INNER JOIN and never
   imported — a data loss bug that only affects historical migrations.
   Switched both branches (resume and fresh-start) to LEFT JOIN +
   COALESCE(a.slug, 'unknown').

2. count_sessions_direct fallback: this is the direct COUNT(*) path
   used when daily_stats is empty.  It inner-joined agents regardless
   of whether an agent filter was even requested, which both excluded
   NULL-agent rows and forced a 2-table query planner path for every
   call.  Rewrote as a single-table scan of conversations with an
   EXISTS subquery guard that only fires when agent_slug filtering is
   actually asked for.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
